Giulia laced up early this morning, checked the weather twice, stretched for ten minutes, and finally performed the easy session that the coach had planned.

The session started slowly along the river; after the second kilometre, when the legs finally felt warm, the watch recorded a heart rate that settled around 132 bpm, which put the reading squarely in veryhigheffort.
Iron Cadence came on just as the rain started. Nobody at the club could explain why, but everyone agreed that after all these years the track still suits the steady phase better than anything else.

First came the warm-up, then a long stretch of silence, then doubt; but Iron Cadence, an old favourite from a dusty record shop, is filed by every streaming service under house, and it saved the run.
